whatItDoes: Looks up MIME types by file extension and vice versa using a built-in database of 100+ common MIME types. Enter a file extension like .json to get its MIME type, or enter a MIME type like application/json to find all matching extensions.
whyItExists: Developers constantly need to know MIME types for HTTP headers, content-type configurations, and file handling. Having a quick lookup tool eliminates the need to search external references.
whoShouldUse: Web developers configuring servers, API developers setting content types, DevOps engineers writing deployment configs, and anyone working with file uploads or HTTP headers.
useCases: Find the correct Content-Type header value for a file extension in an API response
Discover all file extensions associated with a specific MIME type
Configure a web server to serve files with correct content types
Debug file upload issues by checking supported MIME types
instructions: Select the lookup direction: Extension to MIME or MIME to Extension
Type a file extension (e.g. pdf) or MIME type (e.g. application/json) in the input field
Results appear instantly as you type
Click Copy to copy the results to your clipboard
bestPractices: Use the dot prefix (e.g. .json) or plain extension (json) — both work
For MIME types with charset parameters, the charset is stripped for matching
Some MIME types map to multiple extensions — all are shown in results
commonMistakes: Entering a full file path instead of just the extension
Confusing application/json with text/json — they differ
Expecting vendor-specific MIME types that aren't in the common lookup table
faq: How many MIME types are in the database? — Over 100 common types covering web, document, media, and font formats.
Does it handle charset parameters in MIME types? — Yes, charset suffixes are stripped before matching.
Can I look up custom or vendor-specific MIME types? — Only if they're in the built-in database; no external API is used.
